title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 4 (Fragment 4)
text:
Old Niccolo cleared his throat. “Now look here,” he said. Then he stopped. This was Antonio’s business. He looked at Antonio.

Paolo and Tonino noticed that their father looked at their mother first. Elizabeth’s happy look seemed to reassure him a little. “Now, just who are you?” he said to the young man. “How did you meet my Rosa?”

“He was one of the contractors on the Old Bridge, Father,” said Rosa.

“And he has enormous natural talent, Antonio,” said Elizabeth, “and a beautiful singing voice.”

“All right, all right,” said Antonio. “Let the boy speak for himself, women.”

The young man swallowed, and helped the swallow down with a shake of his tie. His face was now very pale. “My name is Marco Andretti,” he said in a pleasant, if husky, voice. “I—I think you met my brother at the bridge, sir. I was on the other shift. That’s how I came to meet Rosa.” The way he smiled down at Rosa left everybody hoping that he would be fit to become a Montana.

“It’ll break their hearts if Father says no,” Lucia whispered to Paolo. Paolo nodded. He could see that.

Antonio was pulling his lip, which was a thing he did when his face could hold no more worry than it did already. “Yes,” he said. “I’ve met Mario Andretti, of course. A very respectable family.” He made that sound not altogether a good thing. “But I’m sure you’re aware, Signor Andretti, that ours is a special family. We have to be careful who we marry. First, what do you think of the Petrocchis?”

Marco’s pale face went fiery red. He answered with a violence which surprised the Montanas, “I hate their guts, Signor Montana!” He seemed so upset that Rosa pulled his arm down and patted it soothingly.

“Marco has personal family reasons, Father,” she said.

“Which I’d prefer not to go into,” Marco said.

“We—well, I’ll not press you for them,” Antonio said, and continued to pull his lip. “But, you see, our family must marry someone with at least some talent for magic. Have you any ability there, Signor Andretti?”

Marco Andretti seemed to relax at this. He smiled, and gently took Rosa’s hands off his sleeve. Then he sang. Elizabeth had been right about his voice. It was a golden tenor. Uncle Lorenzo was heard to rumble that he could not think what a voice like that was doing outside the Milan Opera.

“A golden tree there grows, a tree Whose golden branches bud with green. …”

sang Marco. As he sang, the tree came into being, rooted in the carpet between Rosa and Antonio, first as a faint gold shadow, then as a rattling metal shape, dazzling gold in the shafts of sunlight from the windows. The Montanas nodded their appreciation. The trunk and each branch, even the smallest twig, was indeed pure gold. But Marco sang on, and as he sang, the gold twigs put out buds, pale and fist-shaped at first, then bright and pointed. Instants later, the tree was in leaf. It was moving and rattling constantly to Marco’s singing. It put out pink and white flowers in clusters, which budded, expanded and dropped, as quickly as flames in a firework. The room was full of scent, then of petals fluttering like confetti. Marco still sang, and the tree still moved. Before the last petal had fallen, pointed green fruit was swelling where the flowers had been. The fruit grew brownish and swelled, and swelled and turned bulging and yellow, until the tree drooped under the weight of a heavy crop of big yellow pears.

“… With golden fruit for everyone,”

Marco concluded. He put up a hand, picked one of the pears and held it, rather diffidently, out to Antonio.

vocabulary.definition: The capacity to do something.
vocabulary.example: Have you any ability there, Signor Andretti?

vocabulary.definition: Having knowledge or perception of a situation or fact.
vocabulary.example: “But I’m sure you’re aware, Signor Andretti, that ours is a special family.

vocabulary.definition: Swollen or protruding outwards.
vocabulary.example: The fruit grew brownish and swelled, and swelled and turned bulging and yellow, until the tree drooped under the weight of a heavy crop of big yellow pears.

vocabulary.definition: Groups of similar things or people positioned closely together.
vocabulary.example: It put out pink and white flowers in clusters, which budded, expanded and dropped, as quickly as flames in a firework.

vocabulary.definition: People or companies that undertake work or provide services under a contract.

”

“He was one of the contractors on the Old Bridge, Father,” said Rosa.

vocabulary.definition: The yield of cultivated plants from a particular area.
vocabulary.example: The fruit grew brownish and swelled, and swelled and turned bulging and yellow, until the tree drooped under the weight of a heavy crop of big yellow pears.

vocabulary.definition: Extremely impressive, beautiful, or skillful.
vocabulary.example: As he sang, the tree came into being, rooted in the carpet between Rosa and Antonio, first as a faint gold shadow, then as a rattling metal shape, dazzling gold in the shafts of sunlight from the windows.

vocabulary.definition: In a manner showing shyness or lack of confidence.
vocabulary.example: He put up a hand, picked one of the pears and held it, rather diffidently, out to Antonio.

vocabulary.definition: To bend or hang downwards.
vocabulary.example: The fruit grew brownish and swelled, and swelled and turned bulging and yellow, until the tree drooped under the weight of a heavy crop of big yellow pears.

vocabulary.definition: Very large in size, quantity, or extent.
vocabulary.example: “And he has enormous natural talent, Antonio,” said Elizabeth, “and a beautiful singing voice.

vocabulary.definition: To wave or flap rapidly and lightly.
vocabulary.example: The room was full of scent, then of petals fluttering like confetti.

vocabulary.definition: Made of, resembling, or having the color of gold.
vocabulary.example: It was a golden tenor.

vocabulary.definition: (of a voice) sounding hoarse or rough.
vocabulary.example: “My name is Marco Andretti,” he said in a pleasant, if husky, voice.

vocabulary.definition: To take as a spouse in marriage.
vocabulary.example: We have to be careful who we marry.

vocabulary.definition: An innate ability or skill
vocabulary.example: “And he has enormous natural talent, Antonio,” said Elizabeth, “and a beautiful singing voice.

vocabulary.definition: Relating to an individual or private matters.
vocabulary.example: “Marco has personal family reasons, Father,” she said.

vocabulary.definition: Giving a sense of happy satisfaction or enjoyment.
vocabulary.example: “My name is Marco Andretti,” he said in a pleasant, if husky, voice.

vocabulary.definition: To ask or urge insistently or persistently.
vocabulary.example: “We—well, I’ll not press you for them,” Antonio said, and continued to pull his lip.

vocabulary.definition: Make a rapid succession of short, sharp knocking sounds.
vocabulary.example: As he sang, the tree came into being, rooted in the carpet between Rosa and Antonio, first as a faint gold shadow, then as a rattling metal shape, dazzling gold in the shafts of sunlight from the windows.

vocabulary.definition: To say or do something to remove doubts and fears.
vocabulary.example: Elizabeth’s happy look seemed to reassure him a little.

vocabulary.definition: To become less tense or anxious.
vocabulary.example:
”

Marco Andretti seemed to relax at this.

vocabulary.definition: Regarded by society to be good, proper, or correct.
vocabulary.example: A very respectable family.

vocabulary.definition: A dark area or shape produced by a body coming between light and a surface.
vocabulary.example: As he sang, the tree came into being, rooted in the carpet between Rosa and Antonio, first as a faint gold shadow, then as a rattling metal shape, dazzling gold in the shafts of sunlight from the windows.

vocabulary.definition: Distinguished by a unique characteristic.
vocabulary.example: “But I’m sure you’re aware, Signor Andretti, that ours is a special family.

vocabulary.definition: To increase in size or volume.
vocabulary.example: Before the last petal had fallen, pointed green fruit was swelling where the flowers had been.

vocabulary.definition: A type of classical male singing voice.
vocabulary.example: It was a golden tenor.
